div.div:
After UEFI hands control to grubx64.efi: -
GRUB loads
Reads /boot/grub/grub.cfg
Shows menu
We selection OS
GRUB loads that OS Kernel
GRUB loads initrd (temporary filesystem)
kernel takes over
Mounts real filesystem
starts systemd (process 1)
Desktop appears

div.div: fbx64.efi & BOOTX64.CSV: -It act as a recovery team. If our BIOS boot menu gets completely wiped out, the fbx64.efi program reads that .CSV file. The CSV contains the labels and paths needed to automatically rebuild your BIOS boot entries on the fly.

div.div:
the official UEFI specification dictates that the EFI system partition must use the FAT32
file system. Because FAT32 is a legacy Microsoft creation, the UEFI firmware expects all internal
directory paths to be written using DOS/Windows-style backslashes.

switch.text: the official UEFI specification dictates that the EFI system partition must use the FAT32...
